name: Pakistani Spiced Eggs (Anda)
steps: Soften the onion with the turmeric and jalapeno.
Scramble in the eggs, cooking gently until just set.
steps_detailed: Soften the onion with the turmeric, jalapeno, and cumin in the oil, 5-6 minutes.
Scramble in the eggs, cooking gently, stirring often, until just set - a spiced take on scrambled eggs eaten across Pakistani breakfast tables.
description: A simple spiced scrambled egg breakfast, common across Pakistani households.

name: Pakistani Lentil Dal Breakfast
steps: Simmer the lentils with the cumin, turmeric, garlic, and 1.5 cups water for 20-25 minutes until soft.
steps_detailed: Soften the onion and garlic in the oil, 4-5 minutes.
Add the lentils, cumin, turmeric, and 1.75 cups water, and simmer 22-26 minutes until soft and thickened.
A simple dal like this is eaten at any meal in Pakistan, breakfast included, usually with flatbread.
description: A simple lentil dal, eaten for breakfast as often as any other meal.

name: Pakistani Yogurt with Honey and Cardamom
steps: Stir the honey and cardamom into the yogurt.
steps_detailed: Stir the honey and cardamom into the yogurt and top with the chopped pistachios.
description: A simple, real whole-milk yogurt dish, cardamom being one of the most common Pakistani sweet spices.

name: Pakistani Chicken Karahi
steps: Brown the chicken.
Add the tomatoes, jalapeno, and ginger, and simmer 18-20 minutes until cooked through.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the garlic over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the tomatoes, jalapeno, ginger, and coriander, and simmer 20-22 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has reduced.
Stir in the garam masala off the heat.
description: Karahi, named for the wide, heavy pan it's cooked in, is a fast, tomato-forward everyday dish across Pakistan - a pinch of garam masala at the end gives it real aromatic finish.

name: Pakistani Chana Masala (Chickpea Curry)

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Simmer the chickpeas with the tomatoes, turmeric, and cumin for 15-18 minutes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Soften the onion with the ginger, turmeric, and cumin in a little oil, 5-6 minutes.
Add the chickpeas and tomatoes, and simmer 18-22 minutes, mashing some of the chickpeas to thicken - chana masala is a standard lunch across Pakistan, vegetarian or not.
Stir in the garam masala off the heat.
description: A chickpea curry, finished with a little garam masala for real depth.

name: Pakistani Dal (Lentil Curry)
steps: Soften the onion and garlic with the turmeric.
Add the lentils and 2 cups water, and simmer 25-30 minutes until soft.
steps_detailed: Soften the onion, garlic, cumin, and jalapeno with the turmeric in a little oil, 5-6 minutes.
Add the lentils and 2.25 cups water, and simmer 28-32 minutes until soft and thickened.
Dal, eaten daily across Pakistan, varies enormously by household - this is a plain, everyday version.
description: A simple everyday lentil curry, eaten with rice or bread.

name: Pakistani Chicken Biryani
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion and turmeric.
Add the rice and 1.75 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes.

ingredients_full.note: pinch, steeped in 2 tbsp hot milk

steps_detailed: Marinate the chicken in the yogurt, garlic, ginger, chili powder, and turmeric for at least 30 minutes, or overnight for real depth.
Fry half the onion in the oil over medium heat, stirring often, 10-12 minutes, until deeply browned and crisp; set aside half for the top.
Brown the marinated chicken with the remaining onion in the same oil, 8-10 minutes, along with the cardamom and cinnamon.
Parboil the basmati separately in well-salted water 5-6 minutes, until still quite firm in the center, then drain.
Layer the parboiled rice over the chicken in the pot, drizzle with the saffron milk, scatter with the reserved fried onions and cilantro, cover tightly, and cook over very low heat 20-25 minutes.
Rest 5 minutes off the heat before gently folding the layers together to serve.
description: The actual dum technique biryani is built on - parboiled rice steamed on top of already-cooked, marinated chicken under a tight seal, letting the two layers finish together while staying distinct, rather than being cooked as one pot of seasoned rice from the start. Fried onions (birista), marinated yogurt, and layered steaming are what separate real biryani from a spiced pilaf.

name: Pakistani Beef Keema
steps: Brown the beef with the onion, ginger, and turmeric, breaking it up well, 10-12 minutes.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, ginger, and turmeric, breaking it up well, 11-13 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and simmer 8-10 more minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in the garam masala off the heat.
description: Keema, spiced minced meat, is endlessly versatile, eaten with rice, bread, or folded into other dishes - garam masala stirred in at the end gives real aromatic finish.

name: Pakistani Lamb Karahi
steps: Brown the lamb with the garlic.
Add the tomatoes and jalapeno, and simmer 15-18 minutes until thickened.
steps_detailed: Brown the lamb with the garlic, 8-9 minutes.
Add the tomatoes, jalapeno, ginger, and coriander, and simmer 18-20 minutes until reduced.
Stir in the garam masala off the heat.
description: The lamb version of karahi, finished the same way with garam masala for aromatic lift.

name: Pakistani Chicken Tikka-Style Bake
steps: Marinate the chicken in the yogurt, turmeric, and lime juice for at least 15 minutes.
Bake at 425F for 20-25 minutes until cooked through and lightly charred at the edges.
steps_detailed: Marinate the chicken in the yogurt, turmeric, lime juice, ginger, and garlic for at least 25-30 minutes, or overnight.
Bake at 425F (220C) for 22-28 minutes until cooked through and lightly charred at the edges.
Tikka is traditionally cooked in a tandoor, giving it a distinct smoky char that a home oven can only approximate.
description: The tikka marinade - yogurt, spice, lime - applied to a baked preparation rather than grilled.

name: Pakistani Beef and Lentil Stew (Haleem-Style)
steps: Brown the beef with the ginger.
Add the lentils, bulgur, and 2.5 cups water, and simmer 35-40 minutes, mashing occasionally, until it breaks down into a thick porridge.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the garlic, ginger, and turmeric, 8-10 minutes.
Add the lentils, bulgur, and 2.75 cups water, and simmer 38-42 minutes, mashing occasionally, until it breaks down into a thick, smooth porridge.
Stir in the garam masala off the heat.
description: Haleem, a slow-cooked blend of meat, lentils, and wheat, is beloved across Pakistan and especially prominent during Ramadan - traditionally simmered for hours until completely smooth; this is a faster home version that gets close within a more practical timeframe.

name: Pakistani Samosa-Style Potato Filling
steps: Mix the mashed potato with the cumin, turmeric, and jalapeno.
Warm through in a little oil before serving.
steps_detailed: Mix the mashed potato with the cumin, turmeric, jalapeno, and coriander.
Warm through in the oil, 4-5 minutes, before serving - this is the classic samosa filling, served here on its own rather than wrapped in fried pastry.
description: The spiced potato filling used inside real samosas, served here on its own.

name: Pakistani Raita (Yogurt and Cucumber)
steps: Mix the yogurt, grated cucumber, and cumin together.
steps_detailed: Mix the yogurt, grated cucumber, cumin, and a light dusting of chili powder together.
Raita is served alongside nearly every spiced Pakistani dish to cool the palate.
description: A cooling yogurt side dish, built on real whole-milk yogurt.

name: Pakistani Spiced Chickpea Snack

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Toss the chickpeas with the cumin, chili powder, and lime juice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Toss the chickpeas with the cumin, chili powder, lime juice, and onion.
Let sit 10 minutes so the flavors meld - a common roadside snack across Pakistan, sold in paper cones.
description: A simple spiced chickpea snack.

name: Pakistani Yogurt Naan-Style Flatbread
steps: Mix the flour, yogurt, and baking powder into a soft dough.
Roll thin and cook on a hot, dry griddle, 2-3 minutes per side, until puffed and speckled.
Brush with the butter.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, yogurt, baking powder, and a pinch of salt into a soft, slightly tacky dough, and rest 15-20 minutes.
Roll out thin and cook on a hot, dry griddle, 2-3 minutes per side, until puffed and darkly speckled.
Brush with the butter and a little minced garlic while still warm - yogurt in the dough is what gives naan its characteristic softness and tang.
description: A simple yogurt-enriched flatbread, in the style of naan.

name: Pakistani Rice Pudding (Kheer)
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cardamom and honey for 25-30 minutes until thick.
steps_detailed: Simmer the basmati in the milk with the cardamom over low heat, stirring often, 30-35 minutes, until it breaks down and thickens into a rich, creamy pudding - kheer is cooked longer and reduced further than a Western rice pudding, until the milk itself has visibly thickened, not just the rice.
Stir in the sugar.
Top with the pistachios.
description: Kheer, a rice pudding built on basmati and real cardamom.

name: Pakistani Saffron Rice Dessert
steps: Cook the rice, then stir in the sugar and cinnamon.
Top with the almonds.

ingredients_full.note: pinch, steeped in 1 tbsp hot milk

steps_detailed: Cook the basmati in 1.2 cups water until just tender, 15-18 minutes.
Stir in the sugar, saffron and its milk, and butter while still warm, until the rice turns a deep golden color.
Top generously with the almonds and pistachios.
description: A sweet saffron rice, finished with nuts.

name: Pakistani Honey and Cardamom Sweet
steps: Whisk the cornstarch into a little of the cold milk.
Warm the rest of the milk with the honey and cardamom, whisk in the cornstarch slurry, and simmer until thickened.
steps_detailed: Whisk the cornstarch into a little of the cold milk until smooth.
Warm the rest of the milk with the honey and cardamom, whisk in the cornstarch slurry, and simmer over low heat, stirring constantly, 5-6 minutes until thickened.
Top with the chopped pistachios and chill before serving.
description: A simple milk-based sweet, cardamom-scented.

name: Pakistani Yogurt Mint Sauce
steps: Mix the yogurt, mint, and cumin together.
steps_detailed: Mix the yogurt, mint, cumin, and lime juice together into a bright, cooling sauce - good over grilled meats or kebabs.
description: A simple yogurt-mint sauce, built on real whole-milk yogurt.

name: Pakistani Tomato Ginger Sauce
steps: Soften the garlic and ginger in the oil.
Add the tomatoes and simmer 12-15 minutes until thickened.
steps_detailed: Soften the garlic, ginger, and turmeric in the oil, 2-3 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and simmer 14-16 minutes until thickened - a base sauce underlying much of everyday Pakistani cooking.
description: A simple tomato-ginger sauce base, used across many Pakistani curries.
